title: Can a Pharmacist Prescribe Antibiotics for a UTI in Ontario?
excerpt: Yes, Ontario pharmacists can assess and prescribe antibiotics for an uncomplicated UTI, no doctor's appointment needed. Here's how it works and when to see a physician instead.

title: What Vaccines Can You Get at a Toronto Pharmacy in 2026?
excerpt: Flu, COVID-19, shingles, pneumococcal, RSV, Tdap and travel vaccines: the full 2026 list, who's eligible, and which are free with your Ontario health card.

title: How to Transfer Your Prescription to a New Pharmacy in Toronto
excerpt: Switching pharmacies is easier than you think. We contact your old pharmacy for you, most transfers take about 15 minutes. No paperwork, no hassle.

title: Pink Eye vs. Allergies: How to Tell the Difference
excerpt: Both cause red, watery eyes, but the treatment is completely different. Our pharmacist breaks down the key signs so you don't end up with the wrong drops.

title: What Can a Pharmacist Prescribe in Ontario? The 2026 Minor Ailments Guide
excerpt: Ontario pharmacists can now assess and treat 28 common conditions, from UTIs and yeast infections to pink eye and tick bites, without a doctor's referral. Here's the full list.

title: What Happens During a Medication Review at a Pharmacy?
excerpt: If you take 3 or more medications, a MedsCheck review can uncover interactions, reduce side effects, and simplify your routine, covered by OHIP at most Ontario pharmacies.

title: Vitamins, Probiotics & Herbal Supplements: A Pharmacist's Guide to Natural Health Products
excerpt: Vitamins, minerals, probiotics, and herbal remedies can interact with your prescriptions more than you'd think. Here's what to check before you start one.
